settings.title: Rebrand for Halcyon Skincare
settings.summary: We refreshed a tired skincare brand and unified its packaging, site, and retail display.
settings.description:
Halcyon had grown into a dozen products over five years, and the packaging had drifted into a mix of styles that no longer felt like one brand. Each new product had been designed in the moment, so the shelf looked like a collection of strangers rather than a family.

The brief

The owners did not want to throw away what customers already recognized. They asked for a refresh, not a reinvention, that would pull the range back together and read clearly both in a customer's bathroom and on a store shelf.

What we did

We rebuilt the identity from the wordmark out, fixing the foundation first and then letting everything else follow from it. With the mark, type, and color settled, we carried the new system across the full range so the dozen products finally shared one face.

  • A redrawn wordmark and a tightened color and type system
  • Updated boxes and bottles across all twelve products
  • A refreshed website built on the same system
  • A small in-store display unit for stockists

A real constraint was the bottles themselves. The owners were not reordering glass, so the new labels had to work on the existing containers and within the print runs they had already committed to. We designed the system to fit what they had rather than asking them to start over.

The result

The relaunch gave the line one clear face across every product, the website, and the retail display. The brand reported a noticeable lift in repeat purchases through its own shop, which the owners put down to the range finally looking like a brand worth coming back to.
